workflows.workflowGoal: Guide researchers from concept-driven SAE discovery through semantic feature interpretation to empirical validation of feature-concept relationships.
workflows.description: ConceptViz is explicitly organized around an author-intended three-phase workflow in which researchers first identify a relevant SAE through concept querying and model selection, then interpret candidate features through overview and detail analysis, and finally validate those interpretations through input-based and steering-based checks.
targetUsers: researchers interested in exploring concepts within LLMs through SAE techniques

stages.stageGoal: Identify a concept query and choose an SAE model that best captures the target concept.
stages.description: Researchers formulate and refine a natural-language concept query, inspect its similarity distribution, compare concept relevance across layers, and select the SAE model most aligned with the concept.

usedCoordinations.description: Submitting or refining the query updates the discovery and relevance views used to select an SAE.

stages.expectedOutcome: A refined concept query and a selected SAE model that appears most relevant to the concept.

quotes: First, to efficiently identify relevant SAE models (Identification), we enable concept-based queries combined with structural information about model architecture.
Users first formulate and refine queries to identify potentially relevant SAE models.
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly names Identification as the first workflow phase and links it to concept query and SAE discovery views.

stages.stageGoal: Explore the selected SAE feature space and interpret candidate features in semantic detail.
stages.description: After choosing an SAE, researchers browse the concept-space overview, navigate semantic clusters, select candidate features, and inspect vocabulary projections, activation-similarity evidence, and token summaries to understand feature meaning and limitations.

usedCoordinations.description: Selecting a feature opens the detail views needed for interpretation.
Matrix selection updates the token summary during detailed interpretation.

stages.expectedOutcome: A defensible interpretation of one or more candidate features, including semantic evidence and observed limitations.

quotes: Second, to explore the alignment between feature space and human concepts in-depth (Interpretation), we introduce interactive visualizations that project sparse features into a concept space.
They then navigate through feature spaces to discover and interpret meaningful features related to their concepts of interest.
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly names Interpretation as the second workflow phase and ties it to the explorer and detail views.

stages.stageGoal: Verify whether interpreted features behave consistently and causally with respect to the target concept.
stages.description: Researchers test candidate features on custom inputs, inspect token-level activations, retrieve related features when needed, and compare steered outputs under different activation strengths to confirm or challenge their interpretation.

usedCoordinations.description: The currently selected feature is carried into the validation views.
Custom input submission updates the token-level activation display.
Prompt submission drives the generation of steered output branches.
Selected activated tokens can reveal related features back in the explorer.

stages.expectedOutcome: A validated judgment about whether the feature reliably corresponds to the target concept and produces expected output effects under steering.

quotes: Third, to verify the alignment between features and human concepts at the instance level (Validation), we provide input and output-based validation.
Finally, users validate their understanding by examining how these features activate across different inputs and testing how steering these features affects model outputs.
evidence.reasoning: The Validation phase is explicitly named and is directly realized by the input-activation and output-steering views.

transitions.rationale: After selecting a relevant SAE model, users move from model identification into exploration and interpretation of features within that model; this handoff is operationalized by the coordination that loads the selected SAE into the explorer.

quotes: Users first formulate and refine queries to identify potentially relevant SAE models. They then navigate through feature spaces to discover and interpret meaningful features.
Users can directly select the SAE of interest from this view to proceed with feature exploration.
evidence.reasoning: The intended workflow explicitly sequences model discovery before feature interpretation, and the explorer is entered via the selected-SAE handoff.

transitions.rationale: Once researchers develop an initial interpretation of a feature, the intended next move is to test that interpretation with custom inputs and activation steering.

quotes: Finally, users validate their understanding by examining how these features activate across different inputs and testing how steering these features affects model outputs.
Input Activation View and Output Steering View support the validation of connections between features and concepts.
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly frames validation as the stage used to confirm or challenge interpretations developed in the prior stage.

workflows.expectedFinalOutcome: Researchers build a more accurate mental model of which SAE features correspond to their concepts of interest and how those features behave under observation and intervention.

quotes: ConceptViz implements a novel Identification ⇒Interpretation ⇒Validation pipeline.
This integrated approach ensures a seamless transition from model discovery to detailed analysis and empirical validation.
evidence.reasoning: The workflow is explicitly described at both abstract and system-design level as the intended way to use ConceptViz.

paperLevelClaims.claimText: ConceptViz is intended to support a three-phase concept exploration workflow that moves from identifying relevant SAE models to interpreting features and then validating the resulting concept-feature hypotheses.

quotes: ConceptViz implements a novel Identification ⇒Interpretation ⇒Validation pipeline.
We formulate the concept exploration task into a three-phase workflow.
evidence.reasoning: This claim is directly stated in the abstract and design framing.

quotes: ConceptViz implements a novel Identification ⇒Interpretation ⇒Validation pipeline.
evidence.reasoning: Paper-level intended workflow extraction is grounded in the abstract, workflow framing, task analysis, and system-design description rather than the case studies.
